actors.name: Donald Trump
actors.blurb: Donald Trump remains the central figure in right-wing and far-right news coverage due to his administration's policies, legal battles, and high-profile events. Over the last 48 hours, Trump's actions have driven significant media attention, including his administration's push for stricter immigration policies, legal disputes over voter data access, and his hosting of King Charles III at the White House. The attempted assassination at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has further amplified his prominence in the news cycle, with debates over security measures and political violence dominating discussions.

actors.name: Giorgia Meloni
actors.blurb: Giorgia Meloni continues to shape the news cycle with her government's policies and international engagements. Over the last 48 hours, Meloni has been at the center of debates surrounding Italy's labor reforms, her defense of Justice Minister Carlo Nordio amid the Nicole Minetti pardon controversy, and her stance on EU fiscal rules. Additionally, her diplomatic tensions with Donald Trump over NATO and Iran have drawn significant attention, positioning her as a key figure in both domestic and international right-wing discourse.

actors.name: Santiago Abascal
actors.blurb: Santiago Abascal has been a dominant force in Spanish right-wing news, particularly due to Vox's aggressive push for 'national priority' policies in regional governments. Over the last 48 hours, Abascal's actions have driven coverage, including Vox's clashes with the PP over healthcare and immigration policies, their rejection of rental decree extensions, and their efforts to block migrant regularization. These moves have positioned Vox as a key player in Spain's political landscape, often dictating the terms of debate for the conservative PP.

actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Péter Magyar has rapidly emerged as a defining figure in Hungarian politics following his party's landslide victory in the April 2026 elections. Over the last 48 hours, Magyar's actions have driven significant coverage, including his plans to declassify secret police files, his efforts to reset Hungary's relations with Ukraine, and his appointments to key ministerial positions. His rise marks a dramatic shift in Hungary's political landscape, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule and positioning Magyar as a central figure in both domestic and international right-wing discourse.

actors.name: Marine Le Pen
actors.blurb: Marine Le Pen remains a central figure in French and European right-wing politics, with her actions over the last 48 hours driving significant coverage. Le Pen has been at the forefront of debates surrounding France's nickel strategy in New Caledonia, her party's push for a coordinated EU approach to the region, and her strategic positioning for the 2027 presidential election. Her criticism of Emmanuel Macron's rhetoric and her party's growing influence in the European Parliament have further solidified her role as a key actor in shaping right-wing discourse in France and beyond.

actors.name: Karol Nawrocki
actors.blurb: Karol Nawrocki has emerged as a prominent figure in Polish politics, particularly due to his role in high-profile diplomatic efforts and domestic controversies. Over the last 48 hours, Nawrocki's actions have driven significant coverage, including his involvement in securing the release of journalist Andrzej Poczobut from Belarus, his push for Poland to become a key distributor of U.S. gas in Central Europe, and his decision to declassify a controversial annex to the 2007 WSI report. These moves have positioned Nawrocki as a central figure in both Poland's foreign policy and domestic political debates.

actors.name: Jarosław Kaczyński
actors.blurb: Jarosław Kaczyński remains a central figure in Polish politics, with his actions over the last 48 hours driving significant coverage. Kaczyński has been at the forefront of debates surrounding Poland's cryptocurrency regulations, his party's response to the Zondacrypto scandal, and his criticism of the current government's handling of the Poczobut release. His push for a total ban on cryptocurrencies and his efforts to position PiS as the primary opposition force have kept him in the spotlight, shaping Poland's political discourse.

Over the last 48 hours, Merz's actions have driven significant coverage, including his declining approval ratings, his government's response to the AfD's growing popularity, and his efforts to address Germany's economic challenges. The AfD's lead in polls and Merz's record-low approval ratings have positioned him as a central figure in Germany's political crisis, shaping the country's right-wing discourse.
